title: PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation
exclusions: Does NOT replace the EDR; attests CDE coverage (every CDE host reporting) + that the detection queue is not backed up.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_5_2_edr_attest' (every 30m) emits: hostname, role, edr_seen, last_event, agent_state, queue_backlog, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 5.2 Anti-malware mechanisms — directly evidenced by coverage + health.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 5.2 (Anti-malware mechanisms) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.94: PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.94' running on index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); index=cmdb CDE host list., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.7 Protection against malware — directly evidenced.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.7 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.94: PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.94' running on index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); index=cmdb CDE host list., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 SI-3 Malicious Code Protection — directly evidenced.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 SI-3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.94: PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.94' running on index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); index=cmdb CDE host list., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Stopping the EDR agent on a CDE host for 40m sets edr_seen=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A healthy agent emitting events within 30m is absent.

spl:
| inputlookup cde_hosts.csv
| join type=outer hostname
  [search index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host) earliest=-1h
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_event latest(agent_state) AS agent_state BY hostname]
| eval edr_seen=if(isnotnull(last_event) AND last_event>now()-1800,1,0)
| where edr_seen=0 OR agent_state!="healthy"
| table hostname role edr_seen last_event agent_state
description: Coverage attestation for CDE anti-malware: every CDE host has an EDR event within 30 minutes and the agent is healthy.
value: Converts the annual 'EDR deployed' claim into a 30-minute ground-truth signal — directly auditable against PCI-DSS 5.2 requirement for deployed anti-malware.
implementation: (1) Maintain cde_hosts.csv from CMDB; (2) schedule UC every 30m; (3) gap opens a ticket to the host owner; (4) 24h SLA; (5) weekly CISO report. Indexes required: `index=cmdb`, `index=edr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`, `sourcetype=microsoft:defender:host`.
visualization: Bar chart of coverage by role, table of gaps, single value 'CDE hosts without EDR'.
knownFalsePositives: Hosts under image-build or maintenance windows legitimately show no EDR — use maintenance_calendar.csv.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for coverage attestation for cde anti-malware: every cde host has an edr event within 30 minutes and the agent is healthy. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edr` sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); `index=cmdb` CDE host list..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain cde_hosts.csv from CMDB; (2) schedule UC every 30m; (3) gap opens a ticket to the host owner; (4) 24h SLA; (5) weekly CISO report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup cde_hosts.csv
| join type=outer hostname
  [search index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host) earliest=-1h
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_event latest(agent_state) AS agent_state BY hostname]
| eval edr_seen=if(isnotnull(last_event) AND last_event>now()-1800,1,0)
| where edr_seen=0 OR agent_state!="healthy"
| table hostname role edr_seen last_event agent_state
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation** — Converts the annual 'EDR deployed' claim into a 30-minute ground-truth signal — directly auditable against PCI-DSS 5.2 requirement for deployed anti-malware.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edr` sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); `index=cmdb` CDE host list. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **edr_seen** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where edr_seen=0 OR agent_state!="healthy"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: agent_state, edr_seen, hostname, hostname], last_event, role.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart of coverage by role, table of gaps, single value 'CDE hosts without EDR'.
